---
title: "HRIT Single-ATO Productization"
date: "2026-05-06"
---

**Document:** 22
**Phase:** 6 — HRIT Productization v0.6.0
**Version:** 1.0
**Status:** Draft
**Classification:** Public

**Provenance:**
This document derives from and cites the following canonical sources:

| Canon ID | Title | ADR |
|---|---|---|
| UIAO_140 | Single-ATO Reciprocity Model | ADR-054 |
| UIAO_113 | Evidence Graph | ADR-054 |
| UIAO_143 | HRIT Productization | ADR-058 |
| Spec2-D6.1 | Federal HRIT Integration Runbook | ADR-054, ADR-058 |

All claims in this document trace to one of the above canonical sources.
See [00_CorePrinciples.qmd](00_CorePrinciples.qmd) for the governing SSOT principle.

---

## 1. What This Is

This document narrates the operational productization of the Single-ATO
Reciprocity Model (UIAO_140, ratified by ADR-054).  It answers the question:
*what does it take to run a real system under this model?*

The model itself lives in `src/uiao/canon/specs/single-ato-reciprocity-model.md`
(UIAO_140).  The ADR that ratified the production commitments for this model is
`ADR-058` (`adr-058-hrit-productization-mission.md`).  The federal-civilian
integration runbook that makes the model concrete for OPM HRIT and peer agencies
is `Spec2-D6.1`.

**This document is the narrative gateway** — it introduces the artifacts, shows
how they connect, and points to the KSI family that operationalises continuous
compliance checking across the reciprocity chain.

---

## 2. The Mission

The Single-ATO model operates as follows:

- **One platform, one SSP, one ATO.** The platform authority (e.g. the OPM HRIT
  reference instance under Solicitation 24322626R0007 Amd 4) maintains a single
  System Security Plan and receives a single Authority to Operate from its
  Authorising Official.

- **N consuming agencies, N reciprocity records.** Each agency that wishes to
  operate on the platform acknowledges the controlling SSP, declares any
  configuration elections within the approved latitude table, and receives a
  reciprocity record that binds them to the controlling ATO.  They do not obtain
  a separate ATO.

- **OPM HRIT as the reference instance.** The Federal HRIT modernisation
  programme (Solicitation 24322626R0007 Amd 4) is the first production instance.
  Its consuming agencies include OPM itself, DoD civilian HR, NFC-serviced
  agencies, and IBC-serviced agencies — all defined in the Spec2-D6.1 Federal HR
  Systems Matrix.

This model reduces the ATO surface dramatically: instead of N×ATO processes
running in parallel across agencies, only one ATO is maintained.  Consuming
agencies enter and exit through the reciprocity layer without disturbing the
controlling ATO.

---

## 3. The Artifacts

Four classes of artifact are produced and maintained under this model:

| Artifact | Description | Canon reference |
|---|---|---|
| **Reciprocity record** | Per-agency record that binds the consuming agency to the controlling ATO.  Contains `legal_basis`, `controlling_ato_id`, `configuration_latitude`, `expires_at`, and `acknowledged_by`. | UIAO_140 §4 |
| **Per-agency bundle** | OSCAL evidence bundle (component-definition + assessment-results) generated for each consuming agency.  HMAC-SHA256 signed. | hrit-productization §8 |
| **ATO-cadence telemetry** | Timestamped records of SSP delivery milestones (30-day draft, 45-day final) and reauthorization initiation events. | Spec2-D6.1 §4 |
| **Configuration-latitude findings** | Drift findings raised when a consuming agency's tenant config deviates from the elections in the SSP latitude table. | hrit-productization §5 |

These artifacts are not independent — they form a chain: the ATO-cadence
telemetry gates SSP delivery, the SSP gates the reciprocity record, the
reciprocity record gates the per-agency bundle, and the bundle is the evidence
that consuming-agency Authorising Officials review.

---

## 4. The Evidence Graph

The UIAO evidence graph (UIAO_113 v1.2) represents the causal chain from the
controlling ATO decision through to each consuming agency's reciprocity record.
Every active reciprocity record must be reachable from an `ato-decision` node
via a directed edge; records that are not so anchored are flagged by
KSI-RECIP-008.

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    AO[Authorising Official] -->|issues| ATD[ato-decision node\nControlling ATO]
    ATD -->|edge: ato-decision → reciprocity-record| RR1[reciprocity-record\nAgency 1]
    ATD -->|edge: ato-decision → reciprocity-record| RR2[reciprocity-record\nAgency 2]
    ATD -->|edge: ato-decision → reciprocity-record| RRN[reciprocity-record\nAgency N]
    RR1 --> CA1[Consuming Agency 1\nAO acknowledgement]
    RR2 --> CA2[Consuming Agency 2\nAO acknowledgement]
    RRN --> CAN[Consuming Agency N\nAO acknowledgement]
    ATD -->|conmon evidence attaches here| CM[Continuous-Monitoring\nevidence stream]
    CM -.->|single platform-scope stream| RR1
    CM -.->|single platform-scope stream| RR2
    CM -.->|single platform-scope stream| RRN
```

**Key invariants (UIAO_140 §6):**

1. Continuous-monitoring evidence attaches to the controlling `ato-decision`,
   not to individual reciprocity records.
2. Per-agency bundles are derived views of the platform-scope stream filtered to
   each agency's configuration-latitude elections.
3. Every edge in the graph is directed; the graph is acyclic; root is always
   an `ato-decision` node.

---

## 5. The Lifecycle

A reciprocity record moves through five states from creation to retirement.
The lifecycle is triggered by events in the ATO pipeline, the consuming-agency
onboarding workflow, and the continuous-monitoring stream.

```{mermaid}
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Draft : Agency onboarding request\nreceived; SSP latitude\ntable reviewed
    Draft --> Issued : Authorising Official\nsigns controlling ATO;\nreciprocity record\ngenerated
    Issued --> Active : Consuming-agency CIO\nor delegate provides\nPIV-anchored acknowledgement
    Active --> Expiring : expires_at within 60 days;\nreauthorization window opens
    Expiring --> Active : Renewal record created\n(renewed_from back-ref);\nupdated expires_at set
    Expiring --> Expired : No renewal within 30-day\nSLA (KSI-RECIP-006 FAIL)
    Expired --> [*] : Record archived;\nconsuming agency\noffboarded
    Active --> Expired : AO formally withdraws\nreciprocity grant
```

**Transition triggers:**

| Transition | Trigger | KSI gate |
|---|---|---|
| Draft → Issued | Controlling ATO signed by AO | KSI-RECIP-002 |
| Issued → Active | Consuming-agency CIO acknowledgement (PIV) | KSI-RECIP-001 |
| Active → Expiring | 60-day pre-expiry window opens | KSI-RECIP-003 |
| Expiring → Active | Renewal record created within 30-day SLA | KSI-RECIP-006 |
| Expiring → Expired | 30-day SLA breached with no renewal | KSI-RECIP-006 FAIL |

---

## 6. The KSI Family

The KSI-RECIP family provides continuous automated compliance checking across
all eight operational requirements of the reciprocity model.  Each KSI maps
to one or more NIST SP 800-53 controls and emits a deterministic PASS / WARN /
FAIL verdict that feeds into the platform's FedRAMP continuous-monitoring stream.

| KSI ID | Title | Primary Control | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| KSI-RECIP-001 | Reciprocity Record Legal-Basis Citation Required | AC-21 | HIGH |
| KSI-RECIP-002 | Reciprocity Record References Existing Controlling ATO | CA-2 | HIGH |
| KSI-RECIP-003 | No Expired Reciprocity Records Without Renewal | CA-6 | HIGH |
| KSI-RECIP-004 | Configuration-Latitude Drift Findings Have Remediation Owner | CA-5 | MEDIUM |
| KSI-RECIP-005 | SSP ATO-Cycle Cadence Met (30-Day Draft, 45-Day Final) | PL-2 | HIGH |
| KSI-RECIP-006 | 30-Day Reauthorization Window SLA Met | CA-6 | HIGH |
| KSI-RECIP-007 | Per-Agency Bundle Cryptographic Signature Verified | AU-9 | HIGH |
| KSI-RECIP-008 | Evidence Graph Contains ATO-Decision to Reciprocity-Record Edge | CM-3 | MEDIUM |

KSI rule files are located at:
`src/uiao/rules/ksi/hrit-reciprocity/KSI-RECIP-{001..008}.yaml`

Each rule file follows the ORGTREE-readiness KSI schema pattern:
`ksi_id`, `title`, `category`, `description`, `security_objective`,
`related_controls`, `validation_type`, `evidence_sources`, `pass_criteria`
(with embedded SQL-like logic), `uiao_extensions`, `oscal_props`,
`version`, `last_updated`, `enabled`, `tags`, `severity`, `nist_mappings`,
`fedramp_controls`, and `provenance`.

The KSI family entries are registered in the control-mapping registry at:
`src/uiao/rules/ksi/uiao-control-to-ksi-mapping.yaml` (under `ksi_summary`,
appended 2026-05-06).

---

## 7. How to Walk This

The operational quickstart for HRIT productization is documented in
`docs/hrit-productization-quickstart.md` (produced by WS-A8).  That document
provides:

- Step-by-step agency onboarding flow (Draft → Issued → Active)
- How to issue a reciprocity record via `uiao reciprocity issue`
- How to check KSI compliance via `uiao reciprocity check-ksi`
- How to generate and sign a per-agency bundle
- How to handle expiry and reauthorization

See also:

- `src/uiao/canon/specs/single-ato-reciprocity-model.md` — the governing model
  (UIAO_140)
- `src/uiao/canon/specs/hrit-productization.md` — the productization spec
  (UIAO_143 / ADR-058)
- `src/uiao/canon/specs/Spec2-D6.1-FederalHRITIntegrationRunbook.md` — the
  federal HR systems integration runbook

---

## 8. Bundle Aggregation Flow

The per-agency bundle is assembled from four upstream sources and verified by
the consuming-agency Authorising Official before acceptance.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    SSP[Controlling SSP\nPlatform-scope security plan] --> REC[Reciprocity Record\nAgency elections + legal_basis]
    LAT[Configuration Latitude Table\nSSP-declared tenant elections] --> REC
    TCFG[Tenant Configuration\nActual agency settings] --> DRIFT{Latitude\nDrift Check}
    LAT --> DRIFT
    DRIFT -- No drift --> REC
    DRIFT -- Drift found --> FIND[Config-Latitude Finding\nremediation_owner required\nKSI-RECIP-004]
    FIND --> REC
    REC --> CDEF[OSCAL component-definition\nPlatform components]
    REC --> ARES[OSCAL assessment-results\nContinuous monitoring]
    CDEF --> BUNDLE[Per-Agency Bundle\nbundle_id + bundle_hash]
    ARES --> BUNDLE
    BUNDLE --> SIG{HMAC-SHA256\nSignature}
    SIG -- Verified --> AO_VER[Consuming-Agency AO\nVerification + Acceptance]
    SIG -- Fails --> QUAR[Bundle Quarantined\nKSI-RECIP-007 FAIL]
    AO_VER --> ACTIVE[Reciprocity Record\nstatus = Active]
```

---

## References

- [ADR-054 — Single-ATO Reciprocity Model](../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-054-single-ato-reciprocity.md)
- [ADR-058 — HRIT Productization Mission](../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-058-hrit-productization-mission.md)
- [UIAO_140 — Single-ATO Reciprocity Model Spec](../../src/uiao/canon/specs/single-ato-reciprocity-model.md)
- [UIAO_143 / hrit-productization Spec](../../src/uiao/canon/specs/hrit-productization.md)
- [Spec2-D6.1 — Federal HRIT Integration Runbook](../../src/uiao/canon/specs/Spec2-D6.1-FederalHRITIntegrationRunbook.md)
- OPM Solicitation 24322626R0007 Amendment 4
- [00_CorePrinciples.qmd](00_CorePrinciples.qmd)
- [21_OrgTreeReadiness.qmd](21_OrgTreeReadiness.qmd)
